title: Biosafety and biosecurity compliance activities and enforcement continuum - Canada.ca
content:
## Compliance Activities and Enforcement Continuum
Under the Human Pathogens and Toxins Act and Regulations (HPTA/HPTR) and the Health of Animals Act and Regulations (HAA/HAR)
- Provide information and guidance to stakeholders on their obligations under the HPTA, HPTR, HAA, and HAR
- Publish reports, training materials and run information seminars
- Respond to enquiries in a timely manner
- Maintain an active dialogue with regulated and other interested parties
- Collaborate with federal, provincial/territorial and international organizations
- Engage stakeholders on the development of standards and guidelines
- Develop and publish policies to inform and promote transparency
- Conduct pre-licencing inspections & document reviews

### Monitoring and Verification
- Request, collect and analyse information to assess risk in order to make evidence-based decisions
- Communication of findings
- Issue orders to provide information under HPTA article 38
- Issue human pathogen and toxin licences Footnote 1 , animal pathogen import permits, and HPTA security clearances
- Conduct inspections and document reviews
- Monitor border importations
Administrative Enforcement Activity
- Request corrective actions
- Issue notices of non-compliance
- Refuse to issue, or renew a licence Footnote 1
- Change the conditions and/or terms of a licence Footnote 1
- Suspend a licence Footnote 1
- Revoke a license Footnote 1
- Refuse to issue an HPTA Security Clearance
- Suspend or revoke HPTA Security clearance
Inspection Enforcement Activity
- Recommend the refusal of material into Canada
- Seize and detain material
- Administer forfeitures and disposals of seized materials
- Seize and detain under the Criminal Code (search warrant)
- Conduct investigations
- Recommend cases for prosecution
Administrative Enforcement Activity
- Request corrective actions
- Issue notices of non-compliance
- Refuse to issue, amend or renew permits Footnote 1
- Suspend or cancel a permit Footnote 1
Inspection Enforcement Activity
- Recommend the refusal of material into Canada
- Issue notice of forfeiture, removal or destruction of imported material
- Declaration of infected place (administered by CFIA)
- Issue Administrative Monetary Penalties (administered by CFIA)
- Seize and detain under the Criminal Code (search warrant)
- Conduct investigations (administered by CFIA)
- Recommend cases for prosecution (administered by CFIA)
